title: RestrictionŌĆōslice compatibility i_H^* tau^O_{>=n} = tau^{i_H^*O}_{>=n} is asserted, not proved (keystone of converse induction)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap: the identity $i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}=\tau^{i_H^*\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ is the keystone of the induction and is asserted without proof

In the converse proof the inductive step concludes, for each proper $H\in\mathcal P$,
$$ i_H^* E\ \in\ \tau^{i_H^*\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\ =\ i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}, $$
and **this displayed equality of subcategories of $\mathrm{Sp}^H$ is used verbatim** to feed Lemma `lem:induced` (which requires $i_H^*E\in i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$), thereby producing $E\mathcal P_+\otimes E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$. No proof of the equality is given.

This is logically distinct from the other open issues:
- q5-9/q5-16 concern $\chi^{\mathcal O}(K)=\chi^{i_H^*\mathcal O}(K)$ (a statement about the *combinatorics* of $\chi$);
- q5-6/q5-13 concern the *forward* reduction "suffices to treat $H=G$";
- q5-7/q5-12 mention the issue in passing but treat it as part of "setting up the induction".

Here the precise missing theorem is a **compatibility of restriction with the $\mathcal O$-slice-connective localizing subcategory**:
$$ i_H^*\,\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\ =\ \tau^{i_H^*\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\qquad(\subseteq\mathrm{Sp}^H). $$

**Why it is nontrivial (both inclusions).**

(ŌŖå) The generators of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ are $G_+\otimes_K N^T S^1$ ($K\subseteq G$, $T\in\mathcal O(K)$, $|T|\ge n$). By the Mackey double-coset formula,
$$ i_H^*\bigl(G_+\otimes_K N^T S^1\bigr)\ \simeq\ \bigvee_{[g]\in H\backslash G/K} H_+\otimes_{H\cap{}^gK} N^{\,c_g^* i^*T}\,S^1, $$
and one must check that each wedge summand is a generator of $\tau^{i_H^*\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$: namely that $c_g^* i^* T$ is an $(H\cap{}^gK)$-set *admissible for the restricted system* $i_H^*\mathcal O$ and of cardinality $\ge n$. Admissibility transport requires conjugation-invariance (axiom 2) and restriction-closure (axiom 3) of $\mathcal O$; $|c_g^*i^*T|=|T|\ge n$ uses that restriction along $H\cap{}^gK\hookrightarrow K$ preserves cardinality. None of this is written.

(ŌŖć) One must show $i_H^*$ of the localizing closure on $G$ contains the localizing closure on $H$ of these generators ŌĆö i.e. that $i_H^*$ (a symmetric-monoidal left and right adjoint) carries the localizing subcategory onto the localizing subcategory generated by the images, and that the left adjoint $G_+\otimes_H(-)$ does not enlarge it beyond $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$.

Until this equality is proved, `lem:induced` cannot be invoked with $\tau=\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$, and the downward induction on $|G|$ does not close. This is the single most load-bearing unproved statement in the converse.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Status update on revised solution (critic-agent).** The current `solution.tex` now contains **Lemma `restr` ("the data restricts")**, whose parts (3) and (4) explicitly prove the compatibility this issue flagged:
- (3) $E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\Rightarrow i_H^\ast E\in\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$, via the Mackey double-coset formula for $i_H^\ast(G_+\otimes_L N^SS^1)$ and an explicit per-orbit admissibility check (using axioms (2),(3) on $K_g\cap aJa^{-1}\to K_g$, then conjugation).
- (4) $D\in\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}\Rightarrow G_+\otimes_H D\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$, via associativity of induction.
- (1) $\chi^{\mathcal O|_H}(K)=\chi^{\mathcal O}(K)$ for $K\subseteq H$.

The converse induction is, moreover, deliberately re-routed (Remark `ind-link`) so that it uses only (4)+Lemma `induced` in the direction $i_K^\ast E\in\tau^{\mathcal O|_K}\Rightarrow E\mathcal P_+\otimes E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}$, and does **not** rely on the full identification $i_K^\ast\tau^{\mathcal O}=\tau^{\mathcal O|_K}$. So this keystone now appears **largely addressed**, modulo the global definitional problem (q5-21): the proof of (3)/(4) inherits whatever closure properties $\tau$ actually has, so it should be re-checked once the desuspension issue is fixed.
